generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor streaming point‑cloud data from the 1/10‑scale autonomous vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On‑board ECU motor status sensor providing RPM and temperature"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo position sensor reporting angular setpoint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state sensor reporting speed, heading, and battery level"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance MotorStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatusSensor
]

instance SteeringServoDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point‑cloud frames as received from the vehicle"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor status metrics (RPM, temperature) transmitted to the twin"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering servo angular position values"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle speed, heading, and battery level"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Extracted object tracks generated by Point‑Cloud Clustering & Tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance DesiredSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired speed computed by ACC algorithm for actuation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Point‑Cloud Clustering & Tracking algorithm implemented as a ROS2 node"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm (lead‑vehicle detection, desired‑speed calculation) running on cloud resources"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LowLatencyCommEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low‑latency Wi‑Fi UDP link for end‑to‑end vehicle‑to‑cloud communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware layer aggregating Digital Twin entities and routing messages"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data Translation FE serialises streams, aligns message types and performs unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud computation offloading infrastructure for ACC algorithm"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance GUIVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Graphical user interface that visualises real‑time vehicle state, projected path and object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote management FE allowing operators to enable/disable ACC, set speed and issue emergency brake"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Streams real‑time vehicle state, projected path and detected object tracks to the UI"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs ACC algorithm on cloud and returns desired speed to vehicle for actuation"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedComputationInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency brake"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, SetDesiredSpeedAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into current vehicle speed, heading and battery status"
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected surrounding objects and their predicted trajectories"
]

instance DesiredSpeedComputationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computed desired speed for maintaining safe following distance"
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator command to enable Adaptive Cruise Control"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator sets target cruise speed"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator triggers emergency brake command"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleActuatorComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator FE converting high‑level speed commands from DT into throttle/brake signals"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DTS sends control commands back to the vehicle over the same Wi‑Fi/UDP link. Commands include an ACC enable/disable flag, a desired speed value calculated by the off‑loaded ACC algorithm, and an emergency‑braking command. Command messages are generated by the Controlling FE and transmitted to the vehicle where the actuation FE (Motor, Steering Servo) applies them. The documents do not describe message payload formats, authentication, validation, feedback, or confirmation mechanisms."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires a shared notion of time across devices, imposing a clock‑synchronisation accuracy of 100 ms (the limit set by the hardware). This accuracy governs the latency budget for computation off‑loading and keeps the digital twin temporally aligned with the physical twin."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Orchestration follows the ISO 23247 reference architecture: every Functional Entity is implemented as an individual ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. ROS 2 topics and services interconnect the Physical Twin Entity (sensors, actuators, compute), the Digital Twin Entity (data management, control logic) and the User Entity (3‑D visualization, control panel). Resource ownership resides with the Physical Twin Entity, while the other entities consume these resources through ROS 2 communication, achieving system‑wide coordination."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The architecture mandates a shared notion of time across devices with a clock‑synchronization accuracy of 100 ms, limited by hardware capabilities; safety‑critical vehicular standards may require tighter bounds. No additional model validation, uncertainty quantification, or quality‑assurance processes are described in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Inter‑component communication inside the DTS uses ROS 2. Communication between the physical twin entity and the digital twin entity occurs over a Wi‑Fi network with UDP as the transport protocol. The architecture follows a client–server topology where the vehicle streams sensor data to the DTS and receives control messages in return. The system is required to provide low end‑to‑end latency for safety‑critical ACC functions, but exact bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocol specifications are not provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The ACC service is off‑loaded from the vehicle to the Digital Twin, which may be executed on cloud‑based compute resources (the document references “in the cloud”). No concrete details on the hosting platform, storage capacity, CPU/GPU requirements, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, or availability guarantees are given."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The documents mention OPC Unified Architecture as an example protocol that could be employed for information exchange with external systems, consistent with ISO 23247 part 4 (Information exchange). No specific external integration implementation is detailed for the presented digital twin."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system is built on the ISO 23247 Digital Twin framework for manufacturing, using its entity‑based reference architecture (User Entity, Digital Twin Entity, Physical Twin Entity). The design mentions potential use of OPC Unified Architecture for communication, and notes that many standards for safety‑critical vehicular applications require clock synchronization tighter than 100 ms; the implementation adopts a 100 ms synchronization accuracy due to hardware limits."
]
